summary: Multi-angle review of the COMPLETE PRD V2 — positive lenses (strengths/fun/shippability), negative lenses (attack/break/holes), and reviewer personas (gamedev/balance/new-player/QA) — negatives adversarially verified, then synthesized

synthesis.verdict: NOT-READY (to tag the freeze) — the design spine is genuinely freeze-worthy, but a contained set of confirmed correctness defects (Name-pillar T2 tie-out, estate-build gating, save import-over) plus ~4 unblessed design-intent calls (pace ceiling, the ~60h-vs-28.5h budget headline, Phase-2 novelty, floor-enforcement mechanism) touch LOCKED intent and must be resolved first; none require structural rework.
top_strengths: Sequential two-phase per-tier model (§7.1.4/§3.0.1) is the load-bearing spine done right: every tier gets the SAME build skeleton, the 'half-rungs-maxed-deeds' exploit dies by construction, and the deeds-only-in-Phase-2 invariant is a DERIVABLE state (rung==capstone, no stored flag) the §6.6 verifier can machine-check.

Three clean separately-stored combat tracks (kill→character.level / recognised deed→Arms / curated activity→rung-meter) — the structural fix for v1's fused CombatDeedsPool — restated identically across §1.6.4/§2.8.1/§4.0.1/§6.4.1 and locked by a no-leakage verifier; it lets the auto-battler ship unit-tested at M2a before the Influence economy exists.

M0 front-loads exactly the expensive-to-retrofit foundations (pure-core ESLint boundary as BUILD failures, per-named-stream seeded RNG, multi-backend redundant save, content-verifier+gen:docs) with FALSIFIABLE acceptance checks — a planted Math.random fails the build, a fixed seed yields byte-identical replay.

Pacing is a machine-checkable FLOOR, not vibes: the §4.8.1 T0 table is full-resolution and arithmetic-tied-out (270 min, ~21.5K koku, ≤2× ratios), enforced by an undershoot-ONLY CI gate, and the rung-meter≡time-floor identity drives target, progress bar, and regression off one constant (RUNG_FLOOR_MIN=30).

Textbook minute-one hook: cold open with one verb + a log line (<5s first action, <30s first reveal), feature-unlock-as-plot-beat with ESCALATING named granters (Genemon→Jūbei→Chiyo→Lord), and the signature 'UI grows as you rise' delivered per-beat, not asserted — the project's core differentiator, wired as CI-tested headline beats.

The R3 humbling-first-fight → earned-mastery arc (20–35% win-rate wolf climbing to ~85% over ~30–40 min) is the signature first-session emotional beat, with a clean retreat valve + soft-setback-on-loss giving real stakes-then-relief without souring permadeath.

Determinism discipline is technically correct and is the keystone the whole headless-QA/pacing-gate plan rests on: one seeded RNG as per-named-stream cursors, stateless day-keyed weather, integer-pow-only (banning exactly the non-correctly-rounded IEEE-754 ops while whitelisting sqrt) → byte-identical replay across engines, and active-only enforced structurally (no offline-accrual code path).

Strong authenticity foundation: deep, correct Edo institutional vocabulary (kan'i, toiya, sekisho, daikan/tedai, goyōkin, fudasashi, osso/gimin, the mitate-banzuke) with a period-honest status-rigidity ceiling (yōnin cap, indirect/mediated ascension), plus a real anti-slop UI bible anchored to woodblock pigments.

High fidelity to binding intent: all 79 decisions (incl. the Q48–Q56 deploy cluster) carried with Q-anchors, superseded locks annotated-not-deleted per newest-wins, and the highest-risk V2 reshapes turned into build-failing §6.6.1 invariants rather than prose promises.
blocking: NAME PILLAR T2 TIE-OUT IS BROKEN AND THE PILLAR AUTO-CLEARS THE v1 END-GATE WITH ZERO DEEDS (§4.2.2 L3601/L3610 vs §4.1 L3346 vs §4.8.1b L4387). JUDGE_K[Name][T2]=42,000 is computed off TIER_REF_NAME=140K, not Name's own 28K good band — so the seasonal stream ALONE (42K) is 150% of Name's good band and equals its GREAT band, while §4.2.1/§4.8.1b itemize ZERO Name deeds. The basis clamps f_Name to 1.0 once the other three pillars merely reach good, so Name posts 42K with no player effort: 'good in Name' is vacuous at the climactic T2→T3 gate. This falsifies THREE human-LOCKED statements at once — (a) §4.2.2 L3652 'seasonal is exactly 30% of each good band' (Name=150%, 5× off), (b) the deeds=70%+seasonal=30% split, (c) the breadth-floor/deeds-dominate lock — and the §6.6.1 accrual-tie-out verifier cannot pass for Name without an unstated exemption. §4.2.2 L3601 'Name & Honour gates no v1 tier' also flatly contradicts §4.1, which requires good-in-Name at T2. Same stale-Name-magnitude root cause also breaks §4.2.1's Name cap column (40/400/3.2K implies an 80K good band; both T2 Name deed bases 1.6K/1.8K exceed the §4.1-correct 0.04·28K=1.12K cap, contradicting 'every base ≤ its cap'). Resolution is a human design pick (gate pillar vs derived display bar) — see human_questions.

ESTATE BUILDS E1/E2/E3 ARE GATED ON PILLAR-INFLUENCE FLOORS THAT ARE STRUCTURALLY UNREACHABLE WHERE THE BUILDS ARE PLACED (§4.7.5 L4281-4283, gating text L1331-1332/L4261, vs the Phase-2-only accrual lock FU7/§4.2.1/§4.8.1 L4362-4364). E1@R4 needs Estate≥0.3K and E2@R6 needs Estate≥0.6K+Arms≥0.3K, but those are T0 Phase-1 rungs where Estate/Arms Influence is pinned at 0; E3@G4 needs Estate≥40K+Arms≥15K, but at G4 the pillars are frozen at end-T1 values (≤15K/≤10K) since T2 Phase-2 accrual only opens after G7 — impossible by ~2.7×. The §4.7.5 affordability tie-out validates only the koku COST and silently skips the Influence floors. Affects all three authored v1 builds; needs a human pick (re-gate on the Phase-1 basis vs move builds into Phase 2) — see human_questions.

SAVE IMPORT-OVER CAN BE SILENTLY DISCARDED BY A HIGHER-COUNTER AUTOSAVE (§6.8.1 L6089-6098 newest-wins selector; §6.8 L6044 'import validates magic+migrates'). An imported file (old self-export, QA hand-off, friend's save) carries its own typically-LOWER saveCounter; the spec never says import rebases the counter above the local max nor rewrites all backends, so on the next load the still-present autosave (higher counter) is elected and the player-confirmed import is silently lost. The destructive-action confirm + pre-overwrite snapshot protect the OLD data, not the import; the multi-backend redundancy actively PROPAGATES the loss; and the M0 DoD import test CLEARs the store first, so it passes while the bug stays latent. Violates the save layer's headline 'no silent loss' guarantee on the primary recovery workflow. Related: additive optional-fields WITHOUT a schemaVersion bump (§6.8.2) lets an older/stale-CDN build silently truncate a newer same-version save and newest-wins propagate the loss — the forward-version guard can't catch it because the version didn't change.

trivial_fixes.fix: Change 'so the threshold rises gently while every rung stays ≥ 30 min' → 'so the threshold FALLS gently (fewer activity-points fill the same ≥30-min floor as each activity is worth less per minute) while every rung stays ≥ 30 min'. The formula (30·rate) and the printed table (R1→R7 = 18,19,17,17,17,15,14) both show the threshold FALLING; the wording is the inverse.

Replace 'Name & Honour gates no v1 tier' — it flatly contradicts §4.1 (Name good 28K is REQUIRED at the T2→T3 v1 end-gate) and decisions.md D-008. Restate that Name IS a revealed/gated pillar at T2. (The 42K-vs-8.4K numeric resolution is a human design call; the 'not gated' wording is just wrong and must change regardless.)

'Obaa Sato' → 'Obaa Kuni' — stray pre-rename dead name (Q39 renamed her); §5 T3.7 L5371 already uses the correct 'Obaa Kuni'. Also confirm the §6.6 denylist literal-strings (Obaa Sato / Naozane / Mago / Kuranosuke) so future leaks fail the lint.

Replace bare 'ronin' → 'rōnin' across the data/bestiary/task layers (Q9 requires macronizing rōnin; gōshi is already clean), and REMOVE 'ronin' from the §6.6 macron-lint allow-list (L5921) — the lint currently lists rōnin as both macron-REQUIRED (L5919) and exempt (L5921), so it can never enforce the Q9 fix.

trivial_fixes.where: §4.8.1 R3 row L4352 / M2a phase-5 L6630
trivial_fixes.fix: State explicitly that the scripted first wolf is a GUARANTEED-SURVIVAL beat (win-or-soft-setback), and the LOCKED 20–35% win-rate (§4.8.0) is measured on the post-drill-yard GRINDABLE wolf/sparring encounter — not on the scripted humbling. Removes the build ambiguity sitting on the M2a DoD anchor.

Extend §6.4 settings to include ambientVolume/sfxVolume (and liveRegionScope/paused), OR add a one-line note that §2.21(c) A11ySettings/AudioSettings is the canonical persisted shape — otherwise Q50/Q18-signed audio+a11y prefs don't persist across reload.

Reword 'good in ALL · great in 2–3 · excellent in 1–2' to 'good in ALL revealed; great/excellent COUNTS are per-tier levers that may be 0 (T0: 0 great/1 excellent; T1: 2 great/0 excellent; T2: …)'. As written, T1's authored profile (good in 3 + great in 2, no excellent) violates the 'excellent in 1–2' clause of the predicate TierGate.distributionPredicate claims to enforce. Spec-clarity, not a balance change.

Reconcile the dialogue-RNG contradiction: either DROP 'dialogue flavour' from the §6.7 named-stream list (dialogue stays no-RNG, matching §2.12/§2.18), or ADD a 'dialogue' cursor to the persisted {combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} set. Also state what consumes 'worldgen' in v1 or mark it reserved-unused (E3 is authored, not procedural).

Add one line: 'the app-loop fractional-tick remainder is intentionally ephemeral; GameState is always tick-aligned at save, so replay determinism is unaffected — only sub-tick wall-clock phase resets on load.' Prevents QA filing the resume phase-shift as a determinism regression. (Separately, enumerate CombatEncounterState '{ … }' to confirm the sub-tick attack-speed accumulator + each status's remaining ticks persist, so 'saved fight resumes identically' holds.)

Pin a SINGLE reveal rung for T0 weapon #2 — §7.2.0 lands it at R4 (M2b) while §3.5.1(b) lands '+2 more' at R6. Pick one (e.g. weapon #2 found/crafted at R4 via the loot loop, weapon #3 at R6) and make §3.5.1(b), §7.2.0, and §4.6.9 agree.

human_questions.q: PACE CEILING / OPTIMIZER LOOP: Is the ≥30-min rung floor meant to also cap the CEILING within a rung — i.e. max efficiency and min efficiency clear a rung in the SAME ~30 min, no fast-track even with mastery/gear? §4.1.1 back-solves the meter so 'racing the curated activities still cannot beat the floor', per-event caps forbid bursts, there's no prestige/offline, and the §4.8 gate fails on UNDERSHOOT ONLY — so the genre's central dopamine loop (optimize→go faster, your cited OSRS inspiration) is designed OUT, and 'too boring/too slow' is never machine-detectable. Decide: accept as intended, OR add a within-rung efficiency band (mastery/gear can compress toward a ~20-min faster-floor) + an overshoot/dead-time pacing gate.

BUDGET HEADLINE: Do you accept a ~60h realized v1? §4.8.4 itself shows Phase-1 FLOOR ~28.5h + Phase-2 ADDS ~4.5–5h (T0) + ~12h (T1) + ~15.5h (T2) ≈ +32h ≈ 60h total — ~2× the repeated '28.5h' headline and your stated ~32h target (G1). The Phase-2 half is the content-dry bar-filling. If yes, retitle the headline budget to the realized Phase-1+Phase-2 total EVERYWHERE so 28.5h stops standing in for the real commitment; if no, cut the Phase-2 windows toward ~32h.

PHASE-2 NOVELTY: Phase 2 is the LARGER half of each tier (T2 ~15.5h) yet by FU7 every reveal/weapon/area/tab unlocks in Phase 1 — so Phase 2 injects ~zero new content (T2 adds only cross-pillar combos + the E3 sink), flatlining reveal cadence exactly where the grind is longest. Accept the textbook incremental wall at the widest tier, OR stagger 1–2 genuine reveals into each Phase 2 / interleave Phase-1 rungs with Phase-2 deeds, AND add a Phase-2 novelty fun-proxy to the M6 gate (not just deed-cadence)?

FLOOR ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: The ≥30-min floor (a LOCKED acceptance criterion) has no buildable enforcement — §4.1.1's threshold=30·rate is a tautological back-solve, and the ~14-19 thresholds don't reconcile with the only specified tick model (1-4 ticks/action fills an 18-pt meter in <1 min). The actual lever — a per-activity game-clock tick-cost (or cooldown/availability gating) bounding meter-yield-per-TICK — is unstated, and the §6.6.1 reachability check guards feedability (can you fill it) not un-skippability (can you fill it too fast). Pick the rate-limiting mechanism (high per-activity tick-cost vs availability gating vs hybrid), re-derive the thresholds, and add a max-yield-per-tick ceiling to the verifier — BEFORE code is written against the frozen spec.

HYBRID-GATE BREADTH WALL: The 'good in ALL revealed pillars, no overflow/substitution' rule is checked at the END of a 12-15h Phase 2 — a player who specialises (combat is the fun part) can be 200% over on Arms and still hard-blocked because Estate is 5% under 'good', with zero conversion path. Confirm the breadth-forcing rule is intended to override specialization even when punishing, AND mandate (as a §6.6/fun-proxy requirement, not just Q21 prose) that the pillar UI surfaces ALL revealed gate requirements from the moment Phase 2 opens so players can't over-invest blind.

NAME PILLAR RESOLUTION (resolves blocking #1): Is Name a REQUIRED gate pillar at T2 — then set JUDGE_K[Name][T2]=0.30·28K=8,400 off Name's own good band and author a ~19.6K Name deed inventory (and fix the Name cap column to 0.04·28K=1.12K) — OR a DERIVED display/flavour bar that auto-satisfies 'good' — then formally carve Name out of the 'good in ALL' breadth check, the §4.2.2 L3652 30% statement, and the §6.6.1 verifier? These are materially different games at the climactic v1 gate.

ESTATE-BUILD GATING (resolves blocking #2): Re-gate E1/E2/E3 on the Phase-1 holdings BASIS (the koku-scaled index that grows in Phase 1) and re-derive the floors + the §4.7.5 tie-out, OR move the three builds into each tier's Phase 2, OR define a separate Phase-1 estate-progress measure? As written they read pillar Influence ip and contradict the Phase-2-only accrual lock.

M3 SCOPE: Pre-split M3 at the R6→R7 (Phase-1→Phase-2) seam the way combat was split M2a/M2b? M3 currently fuses the entire four-pillar Phase-2 accrual engine + hybrid gate + House screen with two new nav tabs (Crafting, Quests), two combat reveals, and four rungs R4–R7 — it's the densest single milestone in T0 and the most likely vertical-slice slip; Phase-task 1 alone ('build the four-pillar accrual engine + tests') is milestone-sized. (Same un-split-engine risk applies to M4's silk-market/rep-web and M5's sekisho/combo engines.)

PLAYER-FACING LEGIBILITY: Should v1 ship a single always-reachable 'how the house rises' codex/legend mapping character level vs the two rung-meters vs the four pillars vs House Influence vs the banzuke ranking — plus a per-resolution combat attribution ('+combat XP' / 'counts toward your watch-rank')? The ~6-8-concept naming thicket (Combat Rank meter vs Arms pillar vs combat level; Estate Service meter vs Estate&Wealth pillar) is a real comprehension wall right after the protected first 30 min, and the spec itself keeps flagging conflation as 'the single likeliest regression' — but no glossary/relationship-explainer exists (tooltips are per-item only). Decide whether/how to add it without violating the no-UI-dumps/diegetic-reveal ethos.

FIRST-SESSION SHAPE: R0→R2 is ~60–65 min of pure single-texture labour before combat (the differentiating hook) appears at R3, and two of the seven core fun loops (pillar-climb, seasonal-exam) never fire in the first 2h. For an incremental audience whose first session is often 20-40 min, the entire first session can be labour-only. Accept the slow-burn on-ramp, or seed a combat TASTE / threat foreshadow and one mid-R1 micro-reveal earlier to bridge the 'flat air' window without violating the R3 humbling-fight anchor?

ACTIVE-ONLY vs BACKGROUND THROTTLING: The entire palatability of a ~60h grind rests on 'leave it running, check back' (FU23 tab-open auto-resolve/auto-repeat), but browsers aggressively throttle/freeze background tabs (timer clamp ≥1/min, rAF paused, mobile tabs discarded) — degrading the loop precisely when the player does what it invites, and risking world-clock/season desync against the pacing model. Define the explicit tick policy for hidden/unfocused tabs (Page Visibility + bounded on-refocus catch-up, or a Web Worker tick) and add a background-throttle survival check to M0/M1 profiling. Note a bounded on-refocus catch-up is effectively a small offline grant — confirm that's acceptable vs the active-only lock.

AUTHENTICITY / NAME COLLISION: The lord is 'Munenori' and the sword drillmaster is 'Jūbei' — exactly Yagyū Munenori and his son Yagyū Jūbei, the two most iconic names in samurai sword culture, mapped onto the lord and the sword teacher. The Q27/Q39 fictionalisation sweep covered only surnames, so famous GIVEN names slipped the §6.6 denylist (grep finds zero acknowledgment). Deliberate homage or unintended slop fingerprint to scrub — and should the denylist extend to famous given names? (Renaming the lower-profile 'Munenori' alone breaks the pair cheaply without touching Jūbei's spine creed.)

synthesis.summary:
The Kamikakushi V2 PRD is a genuinely strong, unusually buildable spec whose four V2 spine decisions (sequential two-phase per tier · three separately-stored combat tracks · hybrid breadth gate · incremental combat) are executed cleanly and, crucially, made machine-un-regressable in §6.6.1 rather than left as prose. M0's determinism + redundant-save + pure-core foundation, the arithmetic-tied-out undershoot-only pacing floor, the cold-open hook, and the Edo authenticity/UI-bible work are all high-quality and freeze-worthy. Fidelity to the 79 binding decisions is excellent (Q-anchored, superseded locks annotated-not-deleted).

It is NOT yet ready to TAG as frozen, because freezing locks intent and three confirmed correctness defects plus a cluster of design-intent calls currently touch that intent: (1) the Name pillar's T2 tie-out is broken — its seasonal stream is 150% of its own good band and the pillar auto-clears the climactic v1 end-gate with ZERO deeds, falsifying the LOCKED 70/30 split, the breadth-floor, and the §6.6 verifier (two reviewers found this independently; same stale-Name-magnitude root also breaks the Name deed-cap column); (2) the three authored estate builds E1/E2/E3 are gated on pillar-Influence floors that are structurally unreachable at the Phase-1 rungs where they're placed, contradicting the Phase-2-only accrual lock; (3) a save import-over a higher-counter autosave can be silently discarded by the newest-wins selector, breaking the 'no silent loss' headline on the primary recovery path. All three are localized and have clear resolution paths — but each needs a human pick.

Separately, the most consequential items for the human are design-philosophy calls the spec made implicitly and should bless explicitly before locking: the pace is deliberately capped so the optimizer 'go-faster' loop is designed out (critical), the realized v1 is ~60h not the repeated '28.5h'/~32h headline (high), Phase 2 — the larger half of every tier — reveals nothing new (high), the ≥30-min floor has no actually-buildable enforcement mechanism behind its tautological back-solve (high), and the no-overflow breadth wall can punish a specializer 12-15h deep (high). Recommendation: resolve the 3 confirmed defects, apply the ~10 trivial wording/number fixes, and get a human ruling on the ~5 high/critical design questions; then the spine is ready to freeze without structural rework.

strengths.title: The sequential two-phase per-tier model is the single strongest, most load-bearing design choice
strengths.detail: Phase 1 = climb the ~8 rungs on a per-rung-reset rung-meter AND-gated with story; Phase 2 = the four-pillar grind, which only OPENS after the capstone rung, where pillar DEEDS accrue for the first time. This one choice is load-bearing three ways: (1) it gives every content tier the SAME build skeleton, so M1 stands up the Phase-1 climb once and M3/M4/M5 each just close a tier with its Phase-2 grind + gate — the milestone roadmap literally 'hangs on' it (§7.1.4 final para); (2) it kills the 'half the rungs, maxed deeds' exploit by construction (FU7), not by balance-tuning; (3) the 'deeds accrue only in Phase 2' invariant is a single DERIVABLE check (current rung == R7 capstone — explicitly 'NO extra phase flag', M3 task 1, line 6704), which the §6.6.1 verifier and the M3 DoD ('a run stopped at R6 scores zero') can assert mechanically.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because the entire M1–M5 build cadence, the integrity of the Influence economy, and the deeds-gating invariant all reduce to this one structural rule — and it is expressed as a derivable state, not a flag, so it is machine-checkable.

strengths.title: Three clean separately-stored combat tracks let M2a ship a self-contained, unit-testable auto-battler before the Influence economy exists
strengths.detail: kills/combat-XP → character level; recognised deeds → Arms pillar (Phase-2-gated); per-rung curated activities → Combat Rank rung-meter. De-conflating the old fused CombatDeedsPool is what makes the M2a deliverable real: 'The auto-battler is unit-testable in isolation (no bestiary/equipment dependency)' and the Arms-pillar conversion is deferred to M3. So combat can be built, replayed byte-identically, and win-rate-band-tested at M2a even though pillars/deeds don't land until M3 — the tracks never have to be untangled later.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it decouples the densest engine (combat, M2a/M2b) from the densest economy (Influence, M3); each ships green independently instead of as one entangled slab.

strengths.title: M0 stands up the full architecture + save + determinism spine before any content — the 'narrowest complete game'
strengths.detail: M0 ships: pure-core/renderer split with ESLint boundary rules as BUILD FAILURES (no Math.random/Math.pow/exp/log/trig/DOM/Date.now in core, with the one documented persistence-timestamp exemption); per-named-stream seeded RNG cursors + stateless deriveDayKeyed; reduce/tick contracts; the reveal/event-log bus; the content-verifier + gen:docs from commit #1; and the COMPLETE multi-backend redundant save (IndexedDB+localStorage+sessionStorage, app-identity magic field, monotonic newest-wins selector, additive migration chain, base64 export). DoD includes a byte-identical determinism replay test and < 5 s to first interactable. This front-loads exactly the things that are expensive to retrofit (determinism, save robustness, the core boundary) into commit #1.
strengths.fix: Unusually crisp/buildable: every M0 task has a falsifiable acceptance check (a planted Math.random fails the build; a wrong-app blob rejects to recovery; a fixed seed yields a byte-identical state). De-risks the foundation the whole game sits on.

strengths.title: The §4.8.1 T0 pacing table is a genuine centrepiece — full-resolution, arithmetic-tied-out, and machine-enforced as an undershoot-only floor
strengths.detail: All 8 rungs at full resolution with explicit tie-outs that check (5+30+30+30+35+40+45+55 = 270 min = 4.5 h ✔; lifetime koku 0.75+1.05+1.2+2.1+3.2+4.95+8.25 = 21.5K ≈ the §4.0 band ✔; consecutive cost ratios ≤ ~2× ✔), the R0 cold-open carve-out explicitly blessed, and a CI pacing regression that 'fails on UNDERSHOOT only' (clearing a grind rung < ~28 min fails; overshoot never fails). This turns the LOCKED pacing FLOOR into a machine-checkable acceptance criterion rather than vibes — and the §7.2.0 combat-reveal→milestone map does the same for the combat surface (pins each element to a milestone, no R3 UI-dump).
strengths.fix: Crisp because the headline acceptance target (≥30-min/rung floor, 4.5 h T0) is expressed as a regression test with a defined direction of failure — buildable and unambiguous for the M6 tuner.

strengths.title: The earned-transition spine (§3.2.1) makes every T0 promotion concrete, named, and escalating — narratively buildable
strengths.detail: Every R0→R7 jump has a concrete in-game trigger (rung-meter + story AND-gate), an in-fiction reason ('a dying short-staffed house elevates the proven all-rounder'), and a NAMED granter that escalates Genemon→Jūbei→Chiyo→Lord. The room/area reveals are a parallel data table (RevealableEntry kind:'area') honouring the LOCKED separate-reveal rule. There is no 'rung granted for free' gap for a builder to invent — each beat is a diegetic log line wired to the reveal bus.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing for build legibility: an implementer can author each rung end-to-end (trigger → reveal set → log line → granter) from one row, which is why the milestone note can call a single rung's reveal 'the smallest shippable unit.'

strengths.title: Cold open is a textbook-strong minute-one hook — fast, atmospheric, escalating reveals
strengths.detail: The opening four beats are paced beautifully for first-impression dopamine: first action <5s (rake spilled rice), first meaningful reveal <30s (koku row lights its own panel), and each beat lands an evocative diegetic line ('Cold. Wet straw. The dark smells of old rice. You do not know your name.') before granting a verb. The reveal cadence (log line → 'Open your eyes' verb → body/rest bar + rice counter → koku panel + rest verb + Ranpo) is exactly the 'one verb and a log' minimalism that makes the later UI-growth feel earned. The amnesia is grounded by a physician with ZERO mechanical bonus, honoring the mediocre-start contract while still seeding mystery (the dream fragment). A player is doing something meaningful and being rewarded inside 30 seconds — the single most important window for retention is handled with real craft.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because the first 30 seconds decide whether the player gives the 28.5h grind a chance; the <5s/<30s targets and the atmospheric copy carry the entire first impression and are wired as CI-tested headline beats (§4.8.0).

strengths.title: The R3 humbling-first-fight → earned-mastery arc is the peak dopamine+tension beat of the first session
strengths.detail: Combat going live at R3 (~60–75 min in) is the standout emotional beat: a 20–35% win-rate wolf you survive 'only by luck,' then crawl to Jūbei ('Talent is a story the lucky tell. You are not lucky. So you will work.'). The locked climb from ~25% to ~85% over ~30–40 min of real R3 play is a genuine earned-mastery curve — the player FEELS the training pay off in win-rate, not just a number. The texture is rich for an idle auto-resolver: satiety-gating ('eat before you fight'), a clean RETREAT escape valve (keep HP+loot, never dents Influence), and a soft-setback-on-loss (drop to 1 HP, light injury, never lose levels/gear) together give the fight real stakes-then-relief without the punishing permadeath that would sour a first session. This is the tension-then-relief fun loop (fun-factor §2.6) firing exactly when the player most needs a memorable peak.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: it is the first-session's signature emotional moment and the proof that the mediocre-start premise produces drama rather than frustration; the LOCKED 20–35% anchor plus the visible win-rate climb are what convert 'I got thrashed' into 'I will train.'

strengths.title: Reveal-as-plot-beat + escalating named granters make 'one more rung' narratively magnetic
strengths.detail: Every promotion in the first 2h is a TITLED rank ('another mouth' → day-labourer → bonded hand → yard-hand → trusted hand) granted by a NAMED character whose authority escalates (Genemon → Jūbei → Lady Chiyo → the Lord), each with a concrete in-fiction reason. This turns the meso-loop ('one more rung,' fun-factor §2.2) from a number into a story you want to continue — the goal-gradient pull is reinforced by wanting to hear what the next granter says. The signature 'UI grows' delight also fires early and cleanly: the screen splits from a single column into Work + Skills at R2 ('You begin to notice you're getting better at this'), then a Combat node at R3, a Crafting page at R4 — each its own one-per-beat reveal. Feature unlocks read as plot, never silent menu growth, which is the project's core differentiator and it is delivered, not just asserted.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: the escalating-granter spine is what makes a rise from 'another mouth' to the Lord's bailiff believable inside one tier, and the per-beat nav reveals are the concrete payoff of the 'UI as progression' signature feature in the make-or-break first session.

strengths.title: Three clean combat tracks: "one kill / one deed / one curated activity" maps narrative→data-field→gate-role, restated identically in four sections and locked by a verifier
strengths.detail: The de-conflation of combat into three separately-stored tracks is the design's single most load-bearing elegance. Each track answers a different design question and the system stays teachable because the SAME mnemonic — what 'one kill / one recognised deed / one curated rung activity' writes — appears verbatim at the vision layer (§1.6.4), the systems layer (§2.8.1), the balance layer (§4.0.1) and the data-model layer (§6.4.1), each time pinned to a concrete field: kills→combatXp→`character.level` (the ONLY power-scaling track, driving hpMax/satietyMax/attribute points), recognised deeds→`influence[Arms]` (a Phase-2 gate input), curated activities→`ranks[tier].combatRank` (the per-rung-reset Phase-1 meter). The same combat encounter thus feeds three orthogonal axes — personal power, house standing, rung progress — without ever collapsing into a fused bar, and §6.6.1 asserts each stream sums independently (a kill's XP never reaches Arms or the meter).
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it is the structural fix for the v1 mistake ("Combat Level = a Combat-Deeds pool" that coupled all three). The quadruple-restatement plus a machine-checked no-leakage invariant is what keeps power, standing and pacing from contaminating each other as content scales to ~9-10 weapons and three tiers.

strengths.title: Rung-meter ≡ time-floor: one constant (RUNG_FLOOR_MIN=30) drives the pacing target, the in-game progress meter, AND the CI regression in lockstep
strengths.detail: `thresholdForRung(rung) = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN · eligibleActivityRate(rung)` makes the numeric rung-meter and the ≥30-min pacing floor 'the same constraint expressed two ways.' In most incremental designs these three things — the pacing acceptance target, the player-facing progress bar, and the automated regression — drift apart and are tuned by hand against each other. Here they are derived from one number: the meter threshold is back-solved from the floor × rate, the §4.8 pacing table is tuned to the same floor, and the §6.6 verifier asserts the meter thresholds and the §4.8 curve cannot diverge. Change the floor and all three move together; a balance edit that breaks one fails the build.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it is what lets the PRD claim 'you cannot race past the floor' AND machine-test it — the pacing FLOOR (a human-locked acceptance criterion) becomes a derived consequence of the meter math rather than a separate hand-maintained target.

strengths.title: Pure-core + per-named-stream RNG + integer-pow-only → the DEV play API IS the regression harness; active-only is structural, not a remembered rule
strengths.where: §6.2 module table (L5657-5670); §6.3 determinism guarantee (L5742-5744) + active-only (L5736-5740); §6.7 (L5978-5994); §6.7.1 day-keyed (L6001-6018)
strengths.detail: The determinism commitments compound into one coherent property rather than sitting as separate nice-to-haves. `replay(initialState, [intents], [tickScript])` is byte-identical, which directly makes the play API a pacing/unlock regression harness (not just a debug toy). The per-named-stream cursors {combat, loot, seasonal, worldgen} decouple subsystems so a combat reroll cannot desync loot, yet stay rooted in one seed. Banning Math.pow/exp/log/trig (integer-pow only) buys byte-identical replay ACROSS engines so exported saves are portable. Stateless day-keyed weather/lunar (`deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',day)`) keeps the save minimal and 'can never desync.' And active-only is enforced by the absence of an offline-accrual code path (L5738-5740) — a structural guarantee, not a rule to police. Every one of these reinforces determinism→testability→a machine-checkable pacing floor.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because the entire QA/fun-proxy/pacing-gate strategy (§2.20, §6.10, §4.8 'fails on undershoot') is only valid if replays are deterministic; the integer-pow + single-RNG + minimal-save discipline is the foundation that makes the acceptance criteria testable at all.

strengths.title: "Rewards are one shape everywhere": a single bus fuses narrative scripting and UI-unlock into one dependency graph, so reveals read as plot not menu growth
strengths.detail: Dialogue, dialogue-choices, quests, gathering thresholds and combat all emit the identical `Rewards` object through `core/rewards.applyRewards`, and — the elegant move — story flags AND UI reveals ride that SAME bus, so '"story" and "UI growth" are one dependency graph.' This collapses what is normally two subsystems (a narrative/quest scripting engine + a UI-unlock/visibility engine) into one data-driven funnel, which is exactly what produces the signature 'act → something fades in with a log line' feel. Its corollary is equally clean: reveal staggering is an authored property of the unlock schedule, with NO runtime `revealQueue` field to persist or desync (FU4 supersedes the earlier queue framing).
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it is the mechanism behind pillar 2 (fractal incrementality) and pillar 8 (generated/testable): one bus means a new feature reveal is a data row, automatically narrated, automatically unlock-predicated, and automatically regression-testable — no bespoke wiring per feature.

strengths.title: Budget-as-FLOOR + undershoot-only CI gate dissolves the "long OSRS-rough grind vs automated pacing test" tension that would otherwise need a two-sided band
strengths.where: §4.8 (L4298-4314, gate-fails-on-undershoot L4312); D-029; ties to §4.1.1 back-solve and FU23 auto-repeat (L3422-3423 / L4311-4312)
strengths.detail: Reframing the 28.5h / 30-min-per-rung budget as a FLOOR rather than a target lets the pacing regression fail on undershoot ONLY. This is the quiet keystone that makes the whole 'long grind you leave auto-resolving and check on' vision compatible with an automated gate: a two-sided pass-band would actively punish the rich content-interleaving the design wants, forcing constant retuning. By asserting a minimum, the gate guards the human-locked acceptance criterion (no rung cleared too fast) while leaving the saga free to run longer — and it composes cleanly with the rung-meter back-solve (same 30-min floor) and the tab-open auto-repeat loop (FU23).
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it reconciles three otherwise-conflicting locks — 'long grind' (FU18), 'pacing is machine-tested' (§4.8), and 'content welcome, no quest-type budget' (D-032) — into a single coherent posture instead of a tuning fight.

strengths.title: The hybrid gate's good-band ties out to the FIXED deed inventory, so the gate's own shape manufactures the budget-as-floor behaviour
strengths.detail: The good/great/excellent gate is not an arbitrary set of thresholds — the 'good' band in every revealed pillar is sized to EQUAL exactly what the fixed Phase-2 deed inventory (70%) plus the seasonal stream (30%) delivers. So 'good in all revealed pillars' is precisely the breadth floor every player reaches, while great/excellent are reachable only by pouring the surplus into 1-2 chosen pillars, which 'extends Phase 2 beyond its floor.' The gate geometry (breadth floor + specialise-1-or-2, no overflow) therefore PRODUCES the budget-as-floor intent as a derived consequence, and the verifier's accrual-tie-out asserts the two sides stay consistent (a balance edit on the deed inventory that breaks the re-derived bands fails the build). Gate + deed inventory + pacing floor form one self-consistent triangle.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it is why the gate, the deed content, and the pacing target cannot be tuned in isolation and silently contradict — the 'good = inventory sum' identity is the single constraint that keeps all three honest.

strengths.title: Fractal incrementality + phase-derived-from-rung: one "arrive minimal" aesthetic governs macro and micro, with phase state derived rather than stored
strengths.detail: A single motion — arrive minimal, learn one place/person/verb, everything unlocks — is replayed at every scale: the whole game (kura room → estate → village → region → Edo), each frontier (drill yard: one post → rack → sparring slots), the weapon roster (exactly ONE weapon → ~9-10 over three tiers, one reveal per beat), and the pillar panel (2 revealed at T0 → 3 → 3-4). This is what makes a large system catalog feel like one game rather than a pile of features. It is reinforced by a minimal-state corollary: the Phase-1/Phase-2 marker is DERIVED from the current rung (pre-capstone vs post-capstone), with explicitly 'no stored phase flag' — so the load-bearing 'deeds accrue only in Phase 2' invariant cannot desync from rung state, and the fresh-ladder-per-tier (no continuous staircase) keeps each tier's reveal cadence self-similar.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because the fractal motion is the coherence glue: every new system is introduced by the same reveal grammar (§2.1 bus), so players never face a UI-dump and authors never invent a bespoke onboarding per feature.

strengths.title: The koku master-scale and the rung-meter floor tie out exactly across §4.0 ↔ §4.8.1 ↔ §4.1.1, giving the Arms/Estate/Office spine a machine-checkable backbone
strengths.where: §4.0 (L3234-3235 reconciliation note) & §4.8.1 totals (L4358-4361: R1..R7 = 0.75+1.05+1.2+2.1+3.2+4.95+8.25 = 21.5K ≈ 21K); §4.1.1 (L3391-3404 threshold=30·rate) ↔ §4.8.1 ≥30-min floor; §4.2.2 worked 8-season tie-out (L3625-3651); §6.6.1 accrual/gate-monotonicity verifier (L5943-5949)
strengths.detail: The T0 lifetime-koku band (~21K) is derived as the exact sum of the per-rung koku column, the held-NET figure (~18-19K) is reconciled to it minus the named E1/E2 sinks, the rung-meter thresholds are explicitly back-solved from the same ≥30-min floor the §4.8 pacing table uses, and the seasonal stream is back-solved to exactly 30% of each good band with a per-season worked example — all asserted by the §6.6 verifier. For Arms/Estate/Office this is rigorous, self-consistent, and headlessly regression-testable, which is precisely what keeps a balance retune from silently diverging. (The Name pillar is the lone strand that escaped this discipline — see the critical finding.)
strengths.fix: Load-bearing because it converts the balance intent into CI-enforceable invariants; preserve it and extend the same back-solve discipline to the Name pillar so the verifier covers all four pillars uniformly.

strengths.title: All 79 decisions honoured — incl. the late deploy/presentation items Q48–Q56 in the restored §7.3/§7.4
strengths.detail: The Q48–Q56 cluster the prompt flagged is carried completely and without drift: Q48 functional text→--ink-soft / --ink-faint decorative (§2.21b, M7 task 7); Q50 'good audio' = synth Web Audio + original/CC0 (§7.3.1, §2.21a); Q51 MIT/Apache-2.0 code + reserved content (§7.3.2); Q52 self-hosted OFL fonts, Google subsetting killed (§7.3.1, M7); Q53 itch descriptors child-disappearance/drowning/debt (§7.3.2); Q54 About/Credits + commit-SHA stamp (§2.21.1, §7.3.2); Q55 content/world.ts (§7.3.2); Q56 perf/memory deferred to M0/M1 profiling (R7, L7006). Each carries its Q-anchor in prose. No deploy decision is dropped or contradicted.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: §7.3/§7.4 is the deploy contract M7 builds to; the per-item Q-anchors make it auditable and keep the human-signed deploy intent traceable to source.

strengths.title: The load-bearing V2 decisions are made machine-un-regressable in §6.6.1
strengths.detail: The highest-risk V2 reshapes are turned into build-failing checks rather than prose promises: three-track independence (a kill's XP never reaches Arms/the rung-meter — FU14/Q1), the hybrid gate-distribution good-in-all/great-2-3/excellent-1-2 with combos excluded (Q7/FU10/Q22), trade-≤⅓-holds-post-combo proof (FU20/Q22), gate-monotonicity/ceiling + accrual tie-out (Q28), per-rung-meter reachability (FU6), the per-skill-perk magnitude FLIP from ==0 to small-bounded with conditioning asserted ==0 (Q6/FU8), and the real-name denylist (Q27/Q39/Q11/Q12). This is exactly the 'generate/verify, don't hand-maintain' discipline the project conventions demand.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: these checks are the reason the intent survives the M2–M7 build churn — a balance edit that breaks a human-signed invariant fails verify instead of silently shipping.

strengths.title: Superseded locks are annotated-not-deleted per the newest-wins precedence rule
strengths.where: §1.14 (L1045–1095); §6.13 superseded-resolutions (L6271–6299); D-011/D-016 in ADR log; FU4 callout (L5794–5798)
strengths.detail: Every reversal the precedence rule demands is handled correctly: Q17's runtime reveal-queue → FU4 design-staggered (explicit 'supersedes Q17', NON-field callout in §6.4); the no-labour→combat wall → bounded per-skill perks (relax-notes inline at D-011/D-016/§4.4/§2.7.1); IndexedDB-single-autosave → multi-backend (§6.13 item 1); simple-thresholds-no-floor/overflow → hybrid gate (every occurrence tagged 'superseded', e.g. L2030/L2081/L4529); 'combat Standing'→'Combat Rank' (Q9); '≈halve'→10–15% speedup (H6). No stale framing survives as current canon.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this is the audit guarantee — the binding-intent doc's most-recent-block-wins rule is mechanically reflected in the prose, so a cold reader can't mistake a reversed lock for live canon.

strengths.title: The V2 design spine (sequential two-phase · three combat tracks · hybrid gate · incremental combat) is executed without leakage
strengths.detail: The biggest reshape — de-conflating the old fused 'Combat Level = a Combat Deeds pool' into kills→character.level (HP/attr/satietyMax), recognised deeds→Arms (Phase-2 only), curated activities→Combat Rank rung-meter — is threaded consistently across §2, §3, §4 and the stored data model (ranks.combatRank 'renamed from v1 combatStanding per Q9'; phase marker DERIVED from rung, no stored phase flag per FU7). The weapon table even sums to the stated +2/+3/+4 = 10 (L4148) and is declared byte-identical to §2.8/§2.10/§3. FU7's 'deeds gated to Phase 2' is restated at every rung row to prevent the 'half the rungs, maxed deeds' state.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this de-conflation (FU14/Q1/Q30/Q15) was the single most error-prone V2 change; getting it consistent across catalog, ladder, balance and data-model is what makes M2–M5 buildable from the spec.

strengths.title: The trade-≤⅓ clamp is implemented with a clean, non-circular working form that proves the invariant exactly
strengths.detail: tradeAllowed = 0.5·(land+treasury); trade = min(rawTrade, tradeAllowed); estateTotal = land+treasury+trade. The proof that this yields trade/estateTotal = 1/3 exactly at the cap and <1/3 below is correct and avoids the circular definition (trade ≤ ⅓·estateTotal where estateTotal includes trade) that usually traps this kind of cap. Applied at every accrual point and verifier-enforced post-combo (2305–2307), it makes 'trade can never carry a gate' a genuine structural invariant rather than an aspiration — the one hard cap in the gate model that actually holds under attack.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: keep the 0.5·(land+treasury) working form as the single canonical clamp at every jump/judged accrual point; do not let any later 'convenience' path write the trade strand without re-applying it.

strengths.title: Structural anti-soft-lock guards: fights always terminate, the MC is never weaponless, and the three-track/Phase-2-only design kills the classic incremental exploits
strengths.where: §4.6.3 damage floor (4019–4020), §4.6.1c never-auto-unequip (3998–4002), §4.6.8 retreat valve (4115–4120), §4.0.1 three tracks + Phase-2-only deeds (3288–3299), §4.6.5 kill→XP-only (4052–4060)
strengths.detail: DAMAGE_FLOOR = max(1, 0.10·attackPower) plus the never-auto-unequip 0.55 broken-weapon floor guarantee every auto-resolve fight ends (no infinite stalemate, no weaponless dead-end), and retreat is a clean always-available escape that keeps HP/loot and never dents Influence — so even a 'survived, not won' gate (Hanzaki at G5) cannot soft-lock. Independently, deeds accruing ONLY in Phase 2 (post-capstone, 3295–3299) structurally forecloses the 'half the rungs, maxed deeds' state, and the three-track separation (kill→character-level XP only, not Arms; 4052–4060) means grinding respawn boars cannot balloon the Arms pillar. These are the two soft-lock/exploit classes most likely to bite an incremental game, and both are closed by construction rather than by tuning.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: keep the damage floor, never-weaponless rule, retreat-keeps-progress, and Phase-2-only deed gating as canon — they are the guards that make the long FLOOR grind safe to leave auto-running.

strengths.title: The determinism discipline is correct and is the load-bearing foundation that makes the whole headless-QA plan possible
strengths.where: §6.7 (one RNG, per-named-stream cursors); §6.1/§4.9 (Math.pow/exp/log/trig lint-banned, integer-pow only, sqrt whitelisted); M0 DoD determinism test
strengths.detail: One seeded splitmix64 as per-named-stream monotonic cursors {combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} + stateless day-keyed weather + banning Math.pow/trig in core (using only +,-,*,/,sqrt, which ARE IEEE-754 correctly-rounded and cross-engine-identical, unlike Math.pow) genuinely delivers byte-identical replay. This is what makes seeded fight replay, forced-seed rare outcomes, time-compressed regression, and the pacing gate actually implementable — without it, none of the headless QA spine works. The reasoning (why per-stream cursors, why integer-pow) is sound and unusually well thought through for a design doc.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: every QA/regression/save claim in §6.10, §2.20, qa-playtesting.md depends on this holding. Protect it as a hard lint from commit #1 (M0 already does) and never grant a core exemption beyond the documented persistence-layer Date.now tiebreaker.

strengths.title: Always-shippable cadence + the fixed M2a/M2b combat split + budget-as-FLOOR/undershoot-only gate are a disciplined, measurable build plan
strengths.where: §7.0 cardinal build rule; §7.2 granularity note + M2a/M2b fixed split; §4.8.4 + §7.1.2 (undershoot-only)
strengths.detail: Three good buildability decisions: (1) every milestone is a vertical slice that leaves verify green and the game playable to its frontier, with the smallest unit = one reveal end-to-end — so the build never has a non-working intermediate state; (2) the densest stretch (combat) is pre-split at a natural seam (R3→R4) rather than left as a conditional, removing the single biggest stall risk; (3) reframing the saga budget as a FLOOR with an UNDERSHOOT-ONLY gate converts an open-ended 'tune to exactly 28.5h' trap into a one-sided, measurable target (fail iff too fast), which is both buildable and the right risk posture for an OSRS-rough grind.
strengths.fix: Apply the same fixed-split discipline that worked for combat (M2a/M2b) to the other two unsplit dense milestones (M3 Influence economy, finding #4; and the multi-engine M4/M5, finding #6) — the pattern is already proven in this very plan.

strengths.title: Genuinely well-researched Edo institutional/economic vocabulary, with a status-rigidity-aware ceiling
strengths.where: §1.6.1/§1.7 (kan'i 官威, jikata-yaku, yōnin, rusui-yaku, daikan/tedai 代官所); §5 T2–T4 (toiya, sekisho, sankin-kōtai, goyōkin, fudasashi, osso/gimin, banzuke/mitate); §4.6.9 weapon roster
strengths.detail: The setting is grounded in a deep, correct institutional lexicon rather than generic 'samurai' flavour: koku/mon economy, toiya transport office, sekisho/pass-tiers, daikan-office (代官所) and tedai, jikata-yaku, the rusui-yaku/sankin-kōtai mediated Edo conduit, goyōkin forced loans, fudasashi finance, the osso over-the-head petition borne by a gimin-martyr, and — cleverest — the national 'ranking' rendered as a mitate/parody banzuke (a real Edo print genre, sumo-rank vocabulary) rather than a literal status promotion. Crucially the design respects Edo status-rigidity: a gōshi house could not literally ascend, so recognition is explicitly 'indirect & mediated,' the MC's personal ceiling is capped at yōnin (no hatamoto/shogun audience), and house-vs-personal ascension are decoupled. The weapon roster (yari/kama-yari/nagae-yari/kodachi/uchigatana/ōdachi/bō/naginata/kanabō/tetsubō) is period-accurate and macron-clean. This is the load-bearing authenticity foundation that makes the whole no-magic Edo premise credible.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: the institutional vocabulary + the mediated/indirect ceiling are what keep the 'gōshi house rises to national fame' arc inside period plausibility instead of becoming power-fantasy slop. Preserve the yōnin cap and the mitate-banzuke (not a real rank) framing through any rebalance.

strengths.title: The UI bible is a real anti-slop foundation, not an underspecified/generic palette
strengths.where: ui-design.md §§1–9 (pigment-anchored palette §2; typography §3; key-block/bokashi/hanko §4; anti-slop checklist §9)
strengths.detail: The lens checks whether the woodblock/ink identity is underspecified or generic — it is the opposite. The palette is anchored to real woodblock pigments / the nihon-no-dentōshoku registry (washi kinari, sumi, ai/aizuri indigo, shu vermilion seal, beni, kihada/ochre/rokushō earths), with an explicit discipline ('paper + ink + indigo, one earth per context, vermilion as a stamp not a coat'). It specifies the shuhan key-block frame, bokashi as the ONLY sanctioned gradient, the hanko seal with mix-blend-mode so paper grain shows through the ink, feTurbulence paper grain, significance-gated motion, and a literal §9 anti-slop table (bans purple/blue web gradients, glassmorphism, pill radii, #000-on-#FFF, default shadcn/bootstrap look). The event-log-as-hero and 'UI-as-progression reveal' give it a distinctive, non-generic spine. This is exactly the 'lock the design language first, no screen improvised' discipline the project requires.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this bible is the rubric the visual-QA loop scores against and the strongest single defense against the generic-web-app failure mode. Keep it the gating reference before any UI code; the only real open item is the font pick (already flagged for the human).

strengths.title: 'Combat Standing → Combat Rank' rename applied cleanly and consistently as the live term
strengths.where: Throughout (e.g. §1.6.4 611, §2.15 1567, §3.0.1 2492, §6 5667); every 'Combat Standing' hit sits in explicit rename-framing only
strengths.detail: Every occurrence of 'Combat Standing' in the PRD appears only in 'renamed from'/'renames'/'→' framing — there is no stray LIVE use of the old term — and 'Combat Rank' is used consistently as the martial rung-meter name, with 'Standing' deliberately disambiguated to mean the 官威 Standing & Office pillar ONLY (stated repeatedly, e.g. 611, 1003-1004, 1567). This disambiguation matters because 'Standing' was genuinely overloaded (pillar vs combat gate); the rename and the reserved-word discipline resolve it cleanly across §§1–7.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing for clarity: keep 'Standing' reserved to the 官威 pillar. (Minor watch-item, not a PRD defect: binding-intent FU14 in prd_human_feedback.md still literally writes 'Combat-Standing rung-meter' — under strict most-recent-wins that string could be misread as un-reverting Q9; the PRD correctly treats Q9's rename as intended, but a one-line note in the feedback doc would remove the ambiguity.)

strengths.title: The cold-open onboarding + design-staggered one-reveal-per-beat engine is textbook-strong first-five-minutes and reveal discipline
strengths.where: §3.1 (2591-2607); §4.8.0 (<5s first action, <30s first reveal, 4320-4321); FU4 no-runtime-queue (480); combat-reveal ladder §4.6.9 (4152-4163)
strengths.detail: First action <5s ('rake spilled rice'), first meaningful reveal <30s, one verb and a persistent log at launch, then nav tabs / panels / weapons revealed strictly one-at-a-time on authored beats with NO runtime reveal-queue (FU4 designs the schedule so reveals are inherently sequential). This is the single best retention asset in the doc: the progressive-reveal hook is what carries an incremental's first session, and it's specified with rare rigor (the combat-reveal ladder even retires an earlier 'R3 UI-dump'). This is load-bearing — it's what makes the otherwise-thin first hour survivable and gives the 'what unlocks next' pull the whole genre runs on.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this is the primary first-session retention mechanism and the antidote to the slow combat-at-60min start; protect the <5s/<30s anchors and the one-per-beat law as hard M1 gates, not just prose.

strengths.title: Three-track separation + Phase-2-gated deeds is a clean structural fix to two classic incremental failure modes (conflated-pool, maxed-deeds-at-half-rungs)
strengths.detail: Separating kills→character level (HP/attrs), recognised deeds→Arms pillar, and curated activities→rung-meter — three independently-stored tracks that 'never collapse into one bar', with deeds STRUCTURALLY gated to Phase 2 — pre-empts the conflated 'CombatDeedsPool' it replaced and makes 'half the rungs, maxed deeds' impossible by construction. The fact that 'one kill / one deed / one curated activity' each write to exactly one track, verifier-asserted (§6.6), is the kind of rigor that prevents the silent pacing-curve corruption that kills incrementals mid-development. It's also what keeps the hybrid gate legible (each pillar has one clean accrual source).
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this clean separation is what keeps all three pacing curves independently tunable at M6 without one feeding back into another — preserve the verifier tie-out (§6.6.1) as a hard gate so a future 'just let kills feed Arms a little' shortcut can't re-conflate them.

strengths.title: Cold open is a textbook first-impression: one verb, one log line, zero jargon
strengths.where: §3.1 (cold open table, lines 2596-2607); §5 T0.2 beat 1 (line 4610); fun-factor.md §3 first-5-min hook
strengths.detail: The minute-one experience is exemplary for a confused new player. Launch shows nothing but the event log and a single evocative line; the entire interactable UI is one verb ("Open your eyes" -> "Rake the spilled rice" -> "Lie back and breathe"). There is no nav, no tabs, no map, no numbers to parse beyond a body bar and a rice counter, and every concept is taught by doing one concrete thing. No Edo vocabulary, no meters, no pillars at minute one. This is the strongest possible defense against first-impression overwhelm and directly satisfies the fun-factor doc's "first action <5s, T0 must never *feel* like a tutorial."
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this is the entire first impression and the documented first-5-min hook (fun-factor.md line 153, 274). It is the single thing that decides whether there is a player at all; keep it sacrosanct and resist later pressure to add a verb/panel to this screen.

strengths.title: Progressive disclosure + diegetic reveal keeps the literal first 30 min genuinely gentle
strengths.detail: Nothing appears silently: every panel/tab/area reveals one-at-a-time with an in-fiction log line that explains why it's there ("a feature unlock IS a plot beat, never silent menu growth"). Crucially for the 30-min lens, the pacing floor (~5 min R0, then >=30 min per rung) means a first-30-min player only experiences cold open -> R1 (paddies, labour loop, world clock) and is just approaching R2 (Skills tab). Combat (R3), the pillar panel (R7), and the two parallel meters are all still hidden. The most overwhelming machinery is correctly held back well past the first half hour.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: progressive disclosure is the signature feature AND the comprehension safety net. The pacing floor is what protects the new player from the jargon thicket below; do not let any balance pass pull combat or the Influence panel earlier into the first 30 min.

strengths.title: The integer-pow lint ban is correctly targeted at exactly the non-correctly-rounded IEEE-754 ops, and the sqrt whitelist is sound
strengths.where: §6.1 lines 5567-5572 (ban pow/exp/log/trig, whitelist sqrt); §3.6 line 3621 (judged-result `f = sqrt(frac)`); §4.6.3 line 4018 (`attackPower · rngVariance`)
strengths.detail: IEEE-754 mandates +, −, ×, ÷, and sqrt as correctly-rounded (bit-identical across conforming engines); the transcendentals (pow/exp/log/sin/cos/tan) are NOT required-correctly-rounded and ARE the genuine cross-engine 1-ULP divergence source. Banning exactly those while whitelisting sqrt is precisely right: it keeps the seasonal-appraisal curve (`f = sqrt(frac)`) and the float-heavy combat formulas (`attackPower · rngVariance`, `weatherMod = 1 + 0.10·(2·d−1)`) deterministic because they use only correctly-rounded ops, without forcing those into integer-pow. This is a load-bearing, technically-correct determinism decision rather than cargo-cult.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: it is the difference between 'byte-identical across browsers/OSes' being true vs. aspirational; keep the lint as a build failure (not review) as specified.

strengths.title: RNG-inside-GameState + stateless day-keyed weather + no offline code path makes the save the single source of replay truth
strengths.where: §6.7/§6.7.1 lines 5978-6018; §6.3 lines 5738-5744 (active-only enforced structurally); §6.4 line 1213-1216 (weather/lunar derived, only `day` persists)
strengths.detail: Carrying `{seed, cursors}` IN GameState (not a side object) and re-deriving weather/lunar/festival statelessly from (seed, day) means nothing weather-related can desync from the seed, two saves on the same day reproduce identical weather, and the entire replayable history reduces to (seed, intents, integer-tick-script) — directly Vitest-assertable. The explicit 'no offline-accrual code path' removes the classic idle-game wall-clock-catch-up wormhole that destroys determinism. This is a strong, testable spine that the DEV `__qa` harness can exploit for regression of pacing/unlock timing.
strengths.fix: Load-bearing: this is what lets fixed-seed pacing/reveal regression tests be meaningful and lets force-seed reproduce rare loot/crit outcomes; preserve the 'RNG is part of GameState, never a module singleton' rule.

confirmed.title: Name pillar's seasonal stream (42K) is 150% of its good band, not 30% — the locked 70/30 tie-out is false for Name, and Name auto-clears the T2 gate with ZERO deeds
confirmed.where: §4.2.2 (L3601-3610 JUDGE_K table; L3652 'at every tier the seasonal stream is exactly 30% of each good band') vs §4.1 (L3346 Name T2 good=28K) vs §4.8.1b (L4387 seasonal 'Name 42,000', deed column omits Name) vs §4.2.1 (L3520 T2 itemization omits Name)
confirmed.detail: JUDGE_K[Name][T2]=42,000 is computed off the special normalizer TIER_REF_NAME=140K (0.30·140K), NOT off Name's §4.1 good band of 28K. So Name's seasonal stream alone (42,000) EXCEEDS its entire good band (28K) and equals its GREAT band (42K). Because §4.2.1/§4.8.1b itemize zero Name deeds at T2, Name's only T2 accrual is the 42K seasonal — i.e. Name reaches 'great' with no player effort. This breaks three load-bearing statements at once: (a) §4.2.2 L3652 'the seasonal stream is exactly 30% of each good band' (Name = 150%, off by 5×); (b) §4.1 L3354/L3389 'deeds = 70% + seasonal = 30%, the two add to the good band' (Name = 0% deeds + 150% seasonal); (c) the 'breadth FLOOR every player reaches via the 70/30 grind' / 'deeds dominate' lock (§4.2 L3439). The §6.6.1 'Accrual tie-out' verifier (L3499/L5947) that is supposed to machine-assert this would fail for Name, or has Name silently exempted.
confirmed.fix: Pick one and make it explicit: (1) keep Name derivative+auto-satisfied and DROP Name from the 'good in ALL revealed pillars' breadth check (mark Name as a display/flavour pillar at T2, not a gate input) — and fix §4.2.2 L3652 to say 'except Name, normalized off TIER_REF_NAME'; or (2) make Name a real gated pillar by sizing its seasonal to 0.30·28K=8.4K and adding a 70%·28K=19.6K Name deed itemization. Human question: is Name a required gate pillar at T2 or a derived display bar?
confirmed.verdict:
REAL. Verified from PRD text. JUDGE_K[Name][T2]=42,000 (prd.md L3610) is computed as 0.30·TIER_REF_NAME, where TIER_REF_NAME[T2]=armsGood+estateGood+officeGood=140K (L3583, L3601-3603). Name's T2 good band is 28K (§4.1 L3346), so the seasonal stream (42,000) is 150% of Name's good band and exactly equals its GREAT band (42K). The design's own normalization (f_Name reaches 1.0 at good-band-top, L3572/L3619; basis denominated in full good-band units) forces f_Name=1.0 at the T2 gate (the other three pillars are at/above good there), so Name's seasonal posts 42,000 with ZERO Name deeds. §4.2.1 (L3520-3525) and §4.8.1b (L4387 deed column) itemize zero Name deeds at T2 while the seasonal column reads "Name 42,000". This falsifies three load-bearing, human-locked statements: (a) L3652 "at every tier the seasonal stream is exactly 30% of each good band" (Name=150%, a 5× miss vs the 8.4K that 30%-of-good would be); (b) §4.1 L3354 / §4.8.1b L4389 "deeds=70% + seasonal=30% add to the good band" (Name=0% deeds + 150% seasonal); (c) the deeds-dominate / breadth-floor-via-70-30 lock (§4.2 L3439). The §6.6.1 "Accrual tie-out" verifier (L5947, "deed inventory sums to the gate share") cannot pass for Name (no Name deed inventory vs a 28K good band) without a silent exemption the PRD never states. Net result: Name auto-reaches GREAT and vacuously satisfies its "good in ALL revealed pillars" requirement at the v1 END-gate (T2→T3) with no Name deeds. A secondary contradiction reinforces it: L3601 "Name & Honour gates no v1 tier" vs §4.1 L3323/L3343 which reveal Name at T2 and require "good in ALL" including Name.

The "it's explicitly intended" refutation (L3585-3589) does not save it: even granting the 42K is an intended derivative-basis value, the consequence (auto-great, zero-deed gate-clear, false universal tie-out, broken verifier) is nowhere acknowledged and contradicts the gate semantics in §4.1.

NEEDS HUMAN — not a clean number/wording fix. The PRD embeds two incompatible locked intents and resolving it picks a different game: Option A keep 42K (Name is a derivative pillar that auto-satisfies its T2 "good" — then must amend L3652/§4.8.1b to carve out Name and add an explicit Name exemption to the §6.6.1 verifier); Option B make Name tie out 70/30 like every pillar (set JUDGE_K[Name][T2]=0.30·28K=8,400 off Name's own good band, and itemize ~24 Name deeds = 19,600 in §4.2.1/§4.8.1b). These change whether the player grinds Name at the most important v1 gate, so a human must choose. File: /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md (§4.1 L3346; §4.2.1 L3520/L3527; §4.2.2 L3583/L3586/L3601-3610/L3652; §4.8.1b L4387; §6.6.1 L5947).

confirmed.title: Estate-build gates (E1/E2/E3) require pillar-Influence floors that are structurally unreachable at the Phase-1 rungs where the builds are placed
confirmed.where: §4.7.5 (L4281-4283: E1 'Estate≥0.3K, rank≥R4'; E2 'Estate≥0.6K+Arms≥0.3K, rank≥R6'; E3 'Estate≥40K+Arms≥15K, rank≥G4') + §4.8.1 (L4353 build E1@R4; L4355 build E2@R6) + §4.8.3 (L4438 E3 lands@G4) vs the Phase-2-only accrual lock (§4.8.1 L4362-4364; §4.2.2 L3546; §4.0.1; FU7)
confirmed.detail: Buildings are explicitly placed at Phase-1 rungs (R4, R6, G4 — all pre-capstone). But pillar DEEDS and seasonal CREDIT 'accrue ONLY in Phase 2' (post-capstone): §4.8.1 L4363 states the T0 Estate/Arms deed-ip accrue only in Phase 2, and §4.2.2 L3546 states seasonal credit posts only in Phase 2. Worse at T2: E3@G4 needs Estate≥40K + Arms≥15K, but T2 pillars don't accrue until after G7, so at G4 they are frozen at end-T1 values (Estate ≤15K, Arms ≤10K) — 40K is impossible. §4.7.5's affordability tie-out (L4285-4291) only proves the KOKU cost is reachable and silently ignores the pillar floors. §4.7.5 L4291 even compares the floors to 'T2 good-bands (Estate 60K, §4.1)' which are themselves only reached in T2 Phase 2.
confirmed.fix: Clarify whether the build gate reads the Phase-1 holdings BASIS (the koku-scaled index that grows in Phase 1, §4.2.2 L3547) or the Phase-2-credited pillar ip. As written it reads pillar ip and contradicts the Phase-2-only lock. Either re-state the floors in basis units, or move the builds into Phase 2, or drop the pillar-floor clause for Phase-1 builds (keep koku+rank only).
confirmed.verdict: REAL. The estate builds E1/E2/E3 are gated on the pillar "Influence band" (the credited §4.1 pillar value) — stated explicitly at prd.md L1331-1332 ("gated on Influence band + a LOW rank floor + cost, not the capstone") and L4261 — yet that Influence accrues Phase-2 only (FU7; §4.2.1/§4.2.2 L3546; §4.0.1; §4.8.1 L4362-4364). The builds are placed at Phase-1 rungs: E1@R4 and E2@R6 (T0 Phase 1; capstone R7) and E3@G4 (T2 Phase 1; capstone G7). Consequences confirmed against the band table (§4.1 L3338-3344): in all of T0 Phase 1 Estate=Arms Influence=0, so E1's Estate≥0.3K and E2's Estate≥0.6K+Arms≥0.3K (L4281-4282) are unmeetable; at G4 the pillars are frozen at end-T1 values (Estate excellent=15K, Arms excellent=10K), so E3's Estate≥40K+Arms≥15K (L4283) is unmeetable by ~2.7× on Estate, since T2 Phase-2 accrual only opens after G7. My refutation (that the gate reads the Phase-1-growing holdings/basis from the §4.2.2 reconciliation note rather than the credited Influence) fails: the PRD's explicit gating text says "Influence band," the floors are denominated in ip good-band units, and the §4.7.5 tie-out (L4285-4291) validates only the koku cost while silently skipping the Influence floors — and even compares E3's 40K floor to the T2 good-band (60K), a value reached only in Phase 2 after G4. This is a genuine structural contradiction between two spine-locked principles (Influence-band-gated builds placed pre-capstone vs Phase-2-only pillar accrual), affecting all three authored v1 builds. Needs a human design decision (re-gate builds on the Phase-1 basis and re-derive floors+tie-out, OR move E1/E2/E3 into each tier's Phase 2, OR define a separate Phase-1 estate-progress measure), not a wording fix. Severity high (freeze-blocking but localized to the estate-build gating layer with a clear resolution path), not critical. Key file: /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md (L1331-1332, L4261, L4281-4283, L4285-4291, L4353-4364, L4438-4445; bands L3338-3344).

confirmed.title: Name pillar self-contradicts at T2 and its seasonal stream is mis-scaled to 150% of its own good band — breaking the LOCKED 70/30 split
confirmed.where: §4.2.2 (3601–3610), JUDGE_K table row Name (3610), §4.1 T2→T3 requires Name good 28K (3346)
confirmed.detail: §4.2.2 (3601–3603) states 'Name & Honour gates no v1 tier' and therefore sets JUDGE_K[Name][T2] = 0.30·TIER_REF_NAME = 0.30·140K = 42,000 (3610). But §4.1 (3343–3346) and §2.16 (2051) DO require Name (good 28K) at the T2→T3 v1 end-gate — a direct contradiction. Worse, the scaling breaks the LOCKED deeds-dominate 70/30 invariant for Name: the seasonal budget 42,000 is 1.5× Name's own good band (28K), and at the gate the basis (armsHW+estateHW+officeHW = 30K+84K+78K = 192K) clamps f_Name to 1.0, so the seasonal stream ALONE posts 42,000 ≥ 28K good. Name is thus auto-satisfied as a side-effect of pushing Estate/Office to great, the 'good in Name' requirement is vacuous, and the authored Name deeds (Lord's recognition 1.6K, sponsored rite 1.8K) are redundant for the gate. The §6.6 accrual-tie-out check (which expects each pillar's deeds = 70% of its gate share) cannot tie out for Name.
confirmed.fix: Set JUDGE_K[Name][T2] = 0.30·Name-good (8,400), and feed Name's normalizer/good-band consistently; reconcile the §4.2.2 'Name gates no v1 tier' statement with §4.1's Name requirement at the T2→T3 v1 end-gate (one of them is wrong).
confirmed.verdict:
REAL — reproduced from the PRD text on all three points.

(1) Direct contradiction. §4.2.2 (prd.md:3601, 3610) states "Name & Honour gates no v1 tier" / "— (not gated)", yet §4.1 (3343–3346) lists Name with good 28K / great 42K / excellent 60K in the "T2 → T3 (v1 end-gate)" row under "good in ALL + Estate & Office great/excellent, Arms good," and §2.16 (2051) surfaces Name at T2 (revealed "3–4"). decisions.md D-008 (line 81) locks "upper tiers weight Office + Name" as REQUIRED gating pillars, so §4.1 is canon and the "not gated" line is wrong.

(2) 150% mis-scaling / broken LOCKED 70/30. Every other pillar uses JUDGE_K = 0.30·goodBand with TIER_REF = its good band (3570, 3597), so seasonal telescopes to exactly 30% of good. Name alone uses JUDGE_K[Name][T2] = 0.30·TIER_REF_NAME = 0.30·140K = 42,000, but Name's good band is 28K, so 42K/28K = 1.5 = 150% — exact. At the gate basis armsHW+estateHW+officeHW = 30K+84K+78K = 192K ≥ TIER_REF_NAME 140K, so f_Name clamps to 1.0 and the seasonal stream ALONE posts 42K ≥ 28K good (f_Name actually hits 1.0 once the other three reach merely their good bands, 140K). Name is auto-satisfied by pushing Estate/Office to great; the authored Name deeds (Lord's recognition 1.6K, sponsored rite 1.8K) are redundant for the gate. The 70/30 split is human-SIGNED locked intent (prd_human_feedback H5/G5; decisions.md D-016/D-018, line 181), and it is inverted for Name.

(3) §6.6 accrual tie-out cannot tie out for Name. §4.2.1's 70%-deed itemizations (3504–3525) include only Estate/Arms/Office — Name is absent at every tier, no 70%-of-good Name deed sum exists. The §6.6.1 accrual-tie-out and "good in ALL revealed pillars" gate-distribution checks (3947–3964) cover Name at T2 with no carve-out, so as written the verifier fails on Name or needs an unstated exemption. The PRD's own claims "Name uses the SAME scheme as every other pillar" (3585) and "0.30 applied to the good-band table" (3600) are false for Name (applied to TIER_REF_NAME, 5× the good band).

Severity HIGH: violates a human-signed LOCKED acceptance criterion (70/30) for one of four pillars at the climactic v1 end-gate, makes a required "good in ALL" pillar vacuous, and would break the planned §6.6 content verifier — but it is localized to Name at T2 (does not affect M0/M1/T0/T1 or the spine), so not critical.

NEEDS-HUMAN: the "not gated" wording is a trivial fix, but the real resolution is a design choice among materially different options that each touch locked intent — (A) make Name good = TIER_REF_NAME 140K and author a ~98K Name deed inventory; (B) keep good 28K, cap JUDGE_K[Name][T2] at 0.30·28K = 8.4K and author a ~19.6K Name deed inventory; or (C) formally exempt Name from 70/30 and accept a vacuous "good in Name," which conflicts with the locked "good in ALL revealed pillars / NO overflow / breadth required" rule. Because both the 70/30 split and the hybrid-gate breadth rule are human-signed locked intent, a human must pick.

confirmed.title: The ≥30-min rung floor has no buildable enforcement mechanism — §4.1.1's back-solve is insufficient
confirmed.detail: §4.1.1 gives `thresholdForRung = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN · eligibleActivityRate` → tiny thresholds (~14-19 activity-points, ~30 activities at ~0.6 pts each). Nothing about a ~18-point meter that fills in ~30 dispatches enforces 30 *minutes*. The real floor is a product of FOUR numbers across THREE registries — meter_threshold / meter_yield_per_activity × tick_cost_per_activity × SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN (minutes/tick) — but §4.1.1 hides tick-cost and the tick→wall-clock binding inside the word 'rate' (pts/min). The unstated load-bearing invariant is that EVERY meter-feeding curated activity must carry a tick-cost proportional to its meter yield; if any eligible activity yields meter cheaply in ticks, the floor collapses. An engineer cannot implement '≥30 min' from the formula as written — they'd set threshold=18 and ship a rung clearable in seconds of game-clock.
confirmed.fix: State the floor's actual enforcement: define floor minutes = ticks-to-fill-meter × SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN-per-tick, and make the per-activity tick-cost (not the meter threshold) the locked floor lever. Add the missing invariant ('every rungActivityTag activity's tick-cost is proportional to its meter yield') to the §6.6.1 reachability check. QUESTION for human: is the 30-min floor meant to be enforced by per-activity game-clock cost, by a real-wall-clock action throttle, or by the meter threshold? The three give different implementations and only one is in the text.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL (with one nuance corrected in the claim's favor, not against it). The claim survives adversarial reading.

WHAT I VERIFIED
- §4.1.1 (docs/prd.md:3395-3398): `thresholdForRung = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN(30) · eligibleActivityRate`, with eligibleActivityRate defined as "curated activity-points the rung yields per minute AT THE INTENDED PACE." This is a tautological back-solve: time-to-fill = threshold/rate ≡ 30 min by construction, independent of any real mechanism.
- The thresholds are tiny and openly so: ~14-19 (§4.1.1 table line 3408-3416; §4.8.1 table line 4350-4356; levers index line 4476-4477 "T0 ~14-19"). The text itself calls them "deliberately modest numbers (the floor is enforced by time, not a huge meter)."
- The claim's 4-factor decomposition is mathematically correct. floor_min = (threshold / yield_per_activity) × tick_cost_per_activity × min_per_tick. §4.1.1's single "rate (pts/min)" term silently absorbs BOTH tick_cost (which lives in the activities registry as `baseTicks`, §4.7.1:4203-4209, "1 action ≈ 1-4 ticks" §4.0:3213) AND the tick→wall-clock binding (`SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN[T0/T1/T2]=34/60/120`, 6000 ticks/season — §4.0:3217-3223, §4.8.4:4474). Neither is surfaced in §4.1.1; both are load-bearing for the floor.

THE UNSTATED LOAD-BEARING INVARIANT IS REALLY UNSTATED. The PRD repeatedly ASSERTS the desired OUTCOME ("race the curated activities and the meter is still short of threshold — you cannot skip the floor", §2.15.1:2557, §4.1.1:3401-3403, §1.6.4-area:592) but never states the per-activity constraint that PRODUCES it: that every meter-feeding curated activity must consume game-ticks (or be availability-gated) such that meter-yield-PER-TICK is bounded at the floor's assumed rate. The nearest checks guard the WRONG direction: §6.6.1 "Per-rung-meter reachability" (line 5950) asserts the meter is FEEDABLE (upper bound — can you fill it at all), not UN-SKIPPABLE (lower bound — can you fill it too fast); and the meter being "per-event-capped" (§4.1.1:3398) bounds yield-per-EVENT, not yield-per-TICK, so it does nothing against spamming a cheap-in-ticks activity.

QUANTITATIVE INCONSISTENCY confirming the gap: at T0, 6000 ticks = 34 min ⇒ ~176 ticks/min; normal actions cost 1-4 ticks ⇒ ~44-176 actions/min. A ~18-point threshold at the only tick-cost model the PRD actually specifies (1-4 ticks/action) fills in well under a minute unless each curated activity yields ~0.003-0.01 points — which contradicts the "~30 activities" framing. For the ~18 numbers to mean ~30 min, a curated activity must cost ~176 ticks (≈1 min, ~44-176× a labour action) or be cooldown-gated — a special cost model the PRD never specifies (activities.ts lists curated activities alongside labour nodes with only "yields, skill, season/area gates", §6.5:5870). The rung-meter math lives in pts/min space; the engine lives in ticks/action space; the bridge is missing.

THE STRONGEST DEFENSE, AND WHY IT FAILS. One could argue §4.1.1 is a CALIBRATION rule (measure realized rate from the harness, then set threshold = 30 × measured-rate, with the M6 undershoot CI as the net — §4.8.0:4322, §4.8.4:4459-4462). True that an implementer is not fully stranded (the tick economy + SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN + undershoot CI exist, so nothing ships silently broken). But the formula back-solves from "the INTENDED pace," not the MAXIMUM achievable meter-yield-per-tick. A floor must be derived from the worst case (fastest possible clear), not the expected case; intended-pace only equals max-pace if the curated activities are time-gated — the same unstated invariant. So even the calibration defense reduces to the missing constraint.

WHY needs-human (not trivial-fix): the fix is not a wording/number tweak. It requires (1) a DESIGN choice of the rate-limiting mechanism for curated activities — high per-activity tick-cost vs. availability/cooldown gating vs. hybrid (materially different feel: auto-resolve-spam vs. wait-for-window); (2) re-derivation of the ~14-19 thresholds once that cost model is pinned (they don't reconcile with the 1-4-tick model), rippling into §4.8.1; and (3) a NEW verifier check — a meter-yield-PER-TICK ceiling across the whole eligible set (the cheapest activity included) — since the existing §6.6.1 reachability check guards only feedability. The ≥30-min FLOOR intent is LOCKED by the human (§I-bal/FU18), so only the human can bless the realization mechanism without re-litigating intent.

Severity medium (not high): the invariant is human-LOCKED and double-protected by the M6 undershoot regression, so it cannot silently ship broken, and the surrounding machinery (tick economy, wall-clock binding) exists — an implementer has the pieces and a failing test to guide them. But §4.1.1 as written is genuinely not a buildable enforcement spec, the numbers are internally inconsistent under the only specified tick model, and the actual enforcing invariant is absent — a real, must-resolve gap on the single most important pacing criterion before code is written against the frozen PRD.

confirmed.title: Yagyū father-son name collision: Lord Munenori + drillmaster Jūbei = the two most famous Yagyū swordsmen
confirmed.detail: The estate's lord is 'Munenori' and its sword-drill mentor / master-at-arms is 'Jūbei' (line 703: 'Aging master-at-arms / drillmaster'). These are precisely Yagyū Munenori (1571–1646, shogunal sword instructor) and his son Yagyū Jūbei Mitsuyoshi (the legendary one-eyed swordsman) — the two most iconic names in Japanese sword culture, mapped onto exactly the lord and the sword teacher. To any culturally-literate player this reads as the LLM picking the most famous samurai names, the single most likely authenticity embarrassment in the cast. It is compounded by a separate 'one-eyed mountain god' antagonist (Hanzaki), echoing one-eyed Jūbei, and by drillmaster Jūbei's creed being the tier's emotional spine. The Q27/Q39 fictionalisation sweep only targeted SURNAMES (Toyama/Konoe) and intra-cast collisions, so famous GIVEN names slipped through entirely — no §6.6 denylist entry guards them, and grep finds zero acknowledgment of the Yagyū overlap.
confirmed.fix: Rename at least one (ideally both) — keep Jūbei off the sword-master role especially (e.g. drillmaster → a non-iconic -bei/-zō name; lord Munenori → a non-Yagyū given name). Add Munenori/Jūbei (and a 'famous historical given-name' check, not just surnames) to the §6.6 real-name lint. Human call: is the Yagyū echo deliberate homage or an unintended slop fingerprint to scrub?
confirmed.verdict:
REAL on the facts, but lower-severity than framed; needs a human authenticity/taste call, not a mechanical fix.
